zh.Montfort-le-Gesnois,72241,Sarthe,Pays de: 法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特蒙特福吉萊-洛特盧
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne de MONFORT於大約1250年出生在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特蒙特福吉萊-洛特盧時，她的父親Eudon de Montfort72歲，母親petronille PAYNEL DE HAMBYE52歲。她於1267年與Guillaume VI de PARTHENAY在法國多武-瑟艾福雷斯帕特尼結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1291年3月26日在法國努維勒-阿吉泰多武-瑟艾福雷斯帕特尼享年42歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: MONFORT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Jacques, Marcel, Monique, Wilner.

French and Breton: habitational name from any of numerous places called Montfort, from Old French mont ‘hill’ (see Mont 1) + fort ‘strong, impregnable’ (from Latin fortis). This surname is by far most common in Brittany. It is also found in Haiti. Compare Montfort 1, see also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort, a variant of Montfort 2 and also a cognate of the French name in 1 above and an altered form of its variant Montfort 1 (or perhaps vice versa).

variants.name: Montfort
meanings: French and Breton: variant of   Monfort   1. This surname is most common in Brittany and Haute-Savoie. See also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort: habitational name from Montfoort in Utrecht. It is a cognate of 1 above (see Monfort 1) and also its altered form (or perhaps vice versa).
English (of Norman origin): very rare variant of   Mountford  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Mont
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Juan, Cristina, Domingo, Francisco, Jorge, Luis, Mariano, Ramon, Trinidad.
Catalan and French: topographic name for a mountain dweller, from Catalan and (Old) French mont ‘mountain’ (from Latin mons, genitive montis). Compare French Demont and Dumont .

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne de MONFORT was born about 1250, in Montfort-le-Rotrou, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, her father, Eudon de Montfort, was 72 and her mother, petronille PAYNEL DE HAMBYE, was 52. She married Guillaume VI de PARTHENAY in 1267, in Parthenay, Deux-Sèvres, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 26 March 1291, in Parthenay, Deux-Sèvres, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, at the age of 42.

Elle a épousé Guillaume VI de PARTHENAY en 1267, à Parthenay, Deux-Sèvres, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 26 mars 1291, à Parthenay, Deux-Sèvres, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, à l’âge de 42 ans.

ko.Montfort-le-Gesnois,72241,Sarthe,Pays de: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 몽포트-레-로트루
ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne de MONFORT(이)가 대략 1250년 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 몽포트-레-로트루에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Eudon de Montfort(은)는 72세였고, 그녀의 어머니 petronille PAYNEL DE HAMBYE(은)는 52세였다. 그녀는 1267년 프랑스 데우스-스e브레스 파르테나이에서 Guillaume VI de PARTHENAY(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1291년 3월 26일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 데우스-스e브레스 파르테나이에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne de MONFORT родилась около 1250 г. в местности монтфорт-ле-ротроу, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция, её отцу по имени Eudon de Montfort было 72 года, а матери, которую звали petronille PAYNEL DE HAMBYE, 52. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guillaume VI de PARTHENAY в 1267 г. в местности партенеи, деукс-сeвресь, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 26 марта 1291 г. в местности партенеи, деукс-сeвресь, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция в возрасте 42 лет.
